summary: logger-draft installs persistent WebSocket backdoor disguised as 'MicrosoftSystem64' on postinstall
details: package.json declares `postinstall: node utils.js`, which copies a bundled ~918 KB payload.js (containing the `ws` WebSocket library) into the user's data directory under a `MicrosoftSystem64` folder mimicking Microsoft system components, then installs cross-platform persistence: a systemd --user unit `MicrosoftSystem64.service`, an XDG autostart.desktop file, a Windows scheduled task plus HKCU Run-key fallback, and a detached macOS spawn. The agent is launched with `--agent` and re-spawned detached with `windowsHide:true`/`stdio:'ignore'`. utils.js also bails out early when `os.cpus().length <= 4` to skip developer laptops and small CI runners, and sleeps before acting on non-Windows — sandbox-evasion behavior. The package's advertised purpose is a colorized console logger; the dist/index.js logger is a decoy. None of this behavior is consistent with the stated purpose. Installer harm: any developer or CI host running `npm install logger-draft` gains a hidden long-running WebSocket-controlled background agent persisted across reboots under a deceptive system-component name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
